title: One Product Photo. Every Ad, in Masonry.
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~21s. Featured brand: Cariuma. Inspired by Pixis AI / Adroom (DZIiMehAP9F). Text-and-music driven: no spoken voiceover, the story is carried by typed UI text, big word supers, and the cuts. Full editor brief: on-screen text, the exact Masonry UI element to show per beat, visual, edit, transitions, SFX.
concept: A fast product-demo reel that mirrors the Adroom ad beat for beat, but every screen is Masonry and every product is the Cariuma OCA sneaker. A creator types one line, drops in a single Cariuma product photo, and Masonry builds a finished, on-brand ad: resizes it to any format, turns it into motion, spins up dynamic and lifestyle versions, and fans out a whole campaign in seconds. The payoff: Masonry is the only creative tool the brand needs. The key edit note throughout: wherever the original shows a finished ad or a competing tool, we show the Masonry interface generating that ad from the real shoe, so the reel is its own proof.
audio: No spoken voiceover. Upbeat, modern electronic track with a clear build; cut every super and scene change to the beat. Keyboard-tick foley on the typing beat, soft UI clicks on each Masonry action (generate, resize, animate), a riser into the scale-campaigns beat, one clean hit on the brand reveal.
masonry_about: Masonry is an AI creative studio for brands. You upload your real product, then generate on-brand campaign creative, photo and video, from it: write one prompt and it builds a finished ad, resize that ad to any format, animate stills into motion, Recolor into new colorways, Restyle into new scenes while keeping the exact product, and fan out unlimited variations. THE WHOLE POINT OF THIS EDIT: the inspiration reel keeps cutting to finished ads and to a rival tool's interface. In our version, do not just show a polished product or a finished ad sitting there. Show the Masonry UI element doing it: the prompt bar being typed into, the Cariuma shoe uploaded onto the Masonry canvas, the ad rendering inside Masonry, the format/resize control, the variations grid. The Masonry screen replaces the product shot. That substitution is the message: these ads were made in Masonry, from one photo.
refsummary: The original is a Pixis AI ad for a tool called Adroom. A man types 'Let's create stunning ads,' a single sneaker photo becomes a finished 'Chase the Day' ad with an Order Now button, it resizes to 16:9, shows Design Custom handles, 'Produce Impactful videos,' a dynamic basketball-and-sneaker video, a serum bottle, 'Bulk ads,' 'seconds,' 'Scale campaigns instantly' with creatives fanning out, an athlete and an energy bar ('Fuel every ad with Creative'), then 'Adroom creates the content,' the man pointing at the laptop ('including me'), and 'Start.' It is a text-and-music demo, no spoken voiceover. Watch it once for the typed-then-generated rhythm: every cut is a different thing the tool can spit out. Our version rebuilds that rhythm with Masonry as the tool and Cariuma as the single product running through every ad.
story: A capability-stack arc told through one screen. HOOK: a creator types one line into Masonry, make me a campaign for this shoe. BUILD: drop in one Cariuma product photo and Masonry returns a finished ad, then resizes it to any placement, then turns it into a video, then a dynamic sport cut, then a lifestyle ad, all from the same shoe. SCALE: Bulk ads. Seconds. A whole campaign fans out, every frame on brand. PAYOFF: Masonry makes the content, the creator just directs. CTA: Start. The viewer should believe one product photo plus Masonry equals an entire ad account's worth of creative, with no shoot, no editor queue, no extra budget.
takeaway: From a single Cariuma product photo, Masonry generates a finished ad and then every variation of it (any format, video, dynamic, lifestyle, bulk), on brand, in seconds. It is the only creative tool the brand needs.
script_kind: On-screen text script (no spoken voiceover). These are the words that appear on screen, in order: the typed line and the big word supers. Read top to bottom for the full on-screen copy of the reel.

script.line: One photo in. A finished ad out.
Any product. Same shoe, new scene.
Masonry creates the content. Including the ad you are watching.

beats.super: Line types itself letter by letter inside the Masonry prompt bar, blinking cursor.
beats.visual: A creator at a laptop, calm and confident. We are looking over the shoulder at the screen: the Masonry prompt bar, where the line is being typed.
beats.masonry: Do not show a generic type-stunning-ads text card. Show the real Masonry prompt bar with the line being typed into it, the Cariuma OCA sneaker photo already attached as the reference, cursor blinking, Generate button lit.
beats.edit: Over-the-shoulder push toward the screen as the last word lands.
beats.transition: Cut into the screen on the final keystroke.
beats.sfx: Keyboard ticks under the track; soft click on the cursor.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 0 to 2: the man typing Let's create stunning ads.

beats.ost: One photo in. A finished ad out.
beats.super: finished ad pops bold the instant the ad resolves.
beats.visual: The single Cariuma product photo transforms into a finished, polished ad: shoe on a colored podium, headline, and a clean Shop Now button. It looks agency-made.
beats.masonry: Show this happening on the Masonry canvas: the uploaded shoe photo, then Generate, then the finished Cariuma ad rendering in the result panel. The before and after is the point. The finished ad lives inside Masonry, it is not a standalone graphic.
beats.edit: Quick dissolve from the bare product photo to the rendered ad.
beats.sfx: Soft generate whoosh, button click on Shop Now.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 3 to 4: the sneaker photo becoming the Chase the Day ad with an Order Now button.

beats.visual: The same Cariuma ad snaps from vertical to a wide 16:9 banner, layout intact, nothing stretched.
beats.masonry: Show Masonry's resize and format control being clicked: the same ad reflowing into 16:9 and a 1:1 inside Masonry, product and headline auto-repositioned. Show the format selector UI, not just two finished sizes.
beats.sfx: UI click on the format switch.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 5: the ad shown reformatted to 16:9.

beats.super: Selection handles and bounding boxes blink around the headline.
beats.visual: Editable design layer: text, color, and layout handles around the Cariuma ad, one element nudged to show it is fully editable.
beats.masonry: Show Masonry's edit handles on the live ad: select the headline, recolor a block, drag the logo. The customization happens on the Masonry canvas, not in a separate design app.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 6: the Design Custom frame with bounding-box handles.

beats.super: videos pops with a sparkle as the still starts to move.
beats.visual: The still Cariuma ad becomes motion: subtle parallax, the shoe rotating, light sweeping across it.
beats.masonry: Show Masonry's Animate action on the still: click Animate, the static Cariuma frame starts moving in the result panel. Reveal that the motion was generated in Masonry from the same photo, do not cut to pre-made stock video.
beats.edit: Still to motion on the word videos.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 7 to 8: Produce Impactful videos.

beats.super: Dynamic kinetic, snapping with the cuts.
beats.visual: A high-energy sport cut: the Cariuma shoe mid-air with elements flying past it (a court or street-sport scene), fast and punchy.
beats.masonry: Generate this dynamic scene in Masonry from the same locked shoe: prompt a high-energy sport environment, the shoe stays identical. Show it as a Masonry generation, the shoe is the constant.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 9: the basketballs-and-sneaker dynamic product video.

beats.super: new scene pops as the environment changes.
beats.visual: A premium still-life of the same Cariuma shoe in a totally different look (clean studio, luxe lighting), proving the product holds across any style.
beats.masonry: Use Masonry Restyle: same uploaded Cariuma shoe, new scene and lighting, product unchanged. Show the Restyle control and the consistent product. Where the original cut to an unrelated serum bottle, we keep the one shoe to prove consistency.
beats.edit: Clean cut, let the still breathe.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 10: the cut to a different product (a serum bottle); ours keeps the single Cariuma shoe in a new style.

beats.super: Bulk ads slams in, then seconds fills the frame.
beats.visual: A wall of Cariuma ad variations populating quickly: different headlines, colorways, backgrounds.
beats.masonry: Show Masonry's batch and variations output: one click producing a grid of Cariuma ad variants. The grid is the Masonry results view filling in, not a mockup collage.
beats.edit: Grid fills cell by cell on the beat, then the word seconds punches.
beats.transition: Match cut from one ad to the full grid.
beats.sfx: Rapid ticks as cells fill; one big hit on seconds.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 11 to 12: Bulk ads then seconds.

beats.super: Scale campaigns instantly across the top as cards fan out.
beats.visual: A deck of finished Cariuma creatives fans out across the screen, a hand swiping through them like a campaign rollout.
beats.masonry: Show the fan of cards as Masonry outputs being swiped in the Masonry gallery: every card is a generated Cariuma asset, all on brand. Make clear the whole campaign came out of one Masonry project.
beats.edit: Cards fan with a smooth swipe.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 13: Scale campaigns instantly with creatives fanning out.

beats.super: Creative in a bright accent, energetic.
beats.visual: A lifestyle and active ad: a runner mid-stride wearing the Cariuma shoes, vivid and aspirational, the most lifestyle-forward beat.
beats.masonry: Generate this lifestyle ad in Masonry: prompt an active outdoor scene with a model wearing the same Cariuma shoe. Show it as a Masonry generation with the product locked. Where the original showed an athlete and an unrelated energy bar, ours keeps the shoe and just changes the world around it.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 15 to 16: the running athlete and Fuel every ad with Creative.

beats.ost: Masonry creates the content. Including the ad you are watching.
beats.super: Masonry resolves big and clean; then a wink line including this one.
beats.visual: Brand-reveal beat: the Masonry wordmark resolves; the creator looks at camera and gestures at the screen, owning the punchline.
beats.masonry: This is the substitution made explicit: reveal that every shot in this reel, the ads, the video, the variations, was generated in Masonry. Hold on the Masonry interface with the Cariuma project open behind the wordmark.
beats.edit: Wordmark resolve, then the to-camera gesture.
beats.sfx: One confident hit on the wordmark.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 17 to 19: Adroom creates the content and the man pointing at the laptop, including me.

beats.super: Start centered on a clean end card, Cariuma x Masonry lockup beneath.
beats.visual: End card: a single Start button, the Cariuma x Masonry lockup.
beats.masonry: End on a real Masonry call to action: the Start button as it appears in Masonry, with the Cariuma x Masonry lockup.
beats.edit: Snap to the end card and hold.
beats.sfx: Track resolves on the button click.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 20: the Start CTA card.

notes: Editor workflow: this is a text-and-music reel, no spoken VO, so cut every super and scene change to the track. The single rule that makes this reel work: never show a finished ad or product floating on its own. Always show the Masonry UI element producing it, the prompt bar, the canvas, the resize control, the Animate action, the variations grid, with the one Cariuma OCA sneaker as the product running through every beat (see each beat's Make in Masonry line for exactly which element to show). Load the Cariuma shoe into the Masonry library once and generate all beats from it so the product is identical throughout. The two beats that should be a real Masonry screen-record are the opening prompt-bar type and the finished-ad render. Cariuma is a stand-in demo brand; swap in the real client when producing. No media generated yet: generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
